Cinnamomum, a genus of plants in the family Lauraceae, is a type of evergreen tree or shrub commonly known as cinnamon or cassia. The health benefits of Cinnamomum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum include cinnamaldehyde, cinnamyl acetate, and linalool, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cinnamomum include cinnamon oil, cinnamon powder, and cinnamon tea, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
